purpose: Machine-readable execution queue for autonomous Codex CLI work. Roadmap and TODO files are human-readable views; this file is the queue.
execution_rules: Execute ready items in priority order after checking dependencies.
When a planned item's dependencies become done and no stop condition applies, promote it to ready and continue.

If a feature would otherwise require tenant-specific branching, duplicated provider logic, unsafe tenant data handling, or brittle UI state, promote the smallest useful documented foundation item first.
Do not execute future proposal items unless they are marked ready or are the smallest necessary foundation for a current ready item.
Update this registry status, docs, verification evidence, and run report as each meaningful slice is completed.

items.title: Autonomous execution control plane
items.description: Create the repo-level rules that let a fresh Codex CLI session choose work, resolve doc conflicts, build prerequisites first, verify completion, and continue without relying on hidden context.
acceptance_criteria: Work registry exists and defines status, priority, dependency, stop-condition, safe-default, docs, and verification fields.
Authority order is documented.
Definition of done is documented.
Work registry validation is wired into repo verification.
stop_conditions: Registry cannot be parsed as JSON.
Existing verification cannot run because dependencies are unavailable.
safe_defaults: Prefer documentation and static validation before runtime changes.
Keep future proposals non-executable until marked ready.

items.title: Configuration schema foundation
items.description: Add typed config definitions, tenant overrides, validation, impact preview interface, change history, and undo path for tenant type and tenant configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Config definitions can provide defaults and validation.
Tenant overrides replace defaults without code edits.
Invalid tenant overrides are rejected with plain-language errors.
Config changes are tenant-scoped and auditable.
Impact preview and undo interfaces exist even when a first implementation returns warning metadata.
stop_conditions: A destructive migration is required.
Existing FraterniTees or PICC behavior would be removed.
safe_defaults: Use tenant type defaults when tenant overrides are missing.
Mark unknown provider values as needs_review.
Show warning states instead of authoritative reports when mappings are incomplete.

items.title: Adapter port contracts
items.description: Define ordering, social, and future catalog adapter ports so tenant type features can depend on stable provider boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: OrderingPlatformAdapter contract exists for statuses, orders, customers, contacts, deep links, and sync cursors.
SocialPlatformAdapter contract exists for owned accounts, watched/manual accounts, posts, comments, messages, permission state, and manual fallback.
CatalogAdapter stub exists without profitability behavior.
Contracts use typed input and output validation.
stop_conditions: A provider write-back would be required.
Provider credentials would have to be shared globally.
safe_defaults: Keep provider calls read-only.
Expose missing permissions as explicit adapter state.
Provide manual import fallback for social data.

items.title: Printavo read-only ordering adapter boundary
items.description: Move current Printavo fetch, preview, sync, status, customer, contact, and order behavior behind the ordering adapter contract without changing visible FraterniTees behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Runtime UI and services call the ordering adapter boundary instead of scattering Printavo-specific logic.
Printavo remains read-only.
Existing FraterniTees Printavo sync and lead scoring still work.
Source IDs and source payload references are preserved for debugging.
stop_conditions: Live Printavo write-back becomes necessary.
A required credential is missing and no fixture/manual fallback can prove behavior.
safe_defaults: Use fixtures when live credentials are unavailable.
Preserve current direct code path until adapter parity is verified.
Treat dirty statuses and tags as needs_review.

items.title: Social adapter and manual import foundation
items.description: Create the social adapter boundary and manual adapter implementation so Social monitoring can work before Meta/Instagram permissions are complete.
acceptance_criteria: Owned and watched social accounts can be represented without API credentials.
Manual imports can create posts, comments, messages, and threads with source metadata.
Permission-gated API behavior has explicit unavailable states.
Publishing remains disabled unless feature flagged later.
stop_conditions: A live social provider write-back is required.
Required social credentials are missing and no manual fallback can prove behavior.
safe_defaults: Manual import is allowed.
Publishing is disabled.
Comments and messages show permission-required states until credentials permit access.

items.title: Tenant-scoped Screenprinting feature flags
items.description: Add tenant-scoped feature flags for Screenprinting Sales, Social, publishing, comments, messages, catalog costs, and profitability.
acceptance_criteria: FraterniTees can enable Sales and Social MVP without enabling publishing or catalog costs.
Disabled features do not render broken navigation or empty controls.
PICC is unaffected by Screenprinting feature flags.
stop_conditions: Feature flags would require tenant-specific shared-code branches.
safe_defaults: Sales and Social can be enabled per tenant.
Publishing, comments, messages, catalog costs, and profitability default off until configured.

items.title: Audit and activity hooks
items.description: Add tenant-scoped audit/activity helpers for configuration changes, identity decisions, opportunities, reorders, draft emails, social links, alerts, and manual social logs.
acceptance_criteria: Risky product-owned actions emit tenant-scoped audit or activity records.
Audit/activity records include organization_id and actor metadata when available.
Audit/activity records do not imply provider write-back.
stop_conditions: The audit path would expose cross-tenant data.
safe_defaults: Record internal activity even when provider actions are disabled.
Use system actor metadata when a human actor is unavailable.

items.title: Static boundary and documentation checks
items.description: Extend verification to reject missing work registry fields, lazy requirements, tenant type leaks, unsafe provider credentials, missing organization_id, and missing RLS documentation.
acceptance_criteria: npm run verify includes work registry validation.
Failures explain the broken rule in plain English.
Docs remain self-contained and do not rely on hidden context.
stop_conditions: A check would block current valid repo state without a documented migration path.
safe_defaults: Start with docs and registry validation before deeper static code analysis.
Treat stricter code checks as planned until the repo can pass them.

items.title: Screenprinting additive data foundation
items.description: Add additive Screenprinting tables for mappings, opportunities, reorders, email templates, social accounts, posts, threads, campaigns, alerts, dashboards, and identity resolution.
acceptance_criteria: Every new tenant business table includes organization_id.
RLS is enabled for every new tenant business table.
Migrations are additive and idempotent where practical.
Existing FraterniTees and PICC behavior still passes verification.
stop_conditions: A destructive migration is required.
Existing data would need irreversible transformation.
safe_defaults: Use additive tables and nullable optional fields.
Preserve raw source payloads.
Mark unmapped provider data as needs_review.

items.title: Screenprinting API contracts
items.description: Implement tenant-session-protected Screenprinting API routes for config, mappings, opportunities, reorders, email drafts, social accounts, posts, threads, alerts, dashboards, and identity suggestions.
acceptance_criteria: Routes match docs/API_CONTRACTS.md.
Tenant-mutating routes require tenant session and organization scope.
Validation rejects malformed payloads with clear errors.
No route writes back to Printavo or social providers in MVP.
stop_conditions: A route would need provider write-back.
A route would need direct browser access to tenant tables without RLS policies.
safe_defaults: Return warning metadata when mappings are incomplete.
Use manual social fallback when API credentials are unavailable.

items.title: Screenprinting admin configuration UI
items.description: Build tenant admin configuration for status mapping, payment mapping, tag mapping, dirty field trust, categories, owners, reorder rules, social accounts, alerts, dashboards, and feature flags.
acceptance_criteria: Tenant admins can edit decisions without code changes.
Changes show impact preview or explicit unavailable warning.
Changes affect Sales and Social behavior throughout the app.
Tenant-specific changes do not affect other tenants.
stop_conditions: A required tenant business decision has no safe default.
Admin changes would bypass audit/activity logging.
safe_defaults: Use tenant type defaults until a tenant override is saved.
Use needs_review buckets for dirty or unmapped data.
Expose expert JSON only after safe form controls exist.

items.title: Screenprinting Sales MVP
items.description: Build the Screenprinting Sales workspace: dashboards, orders, accounts cleanup, opportunities, reorders, draft email templates, goals, and reporting while preserving FraterniTees lead scoring.
acceptance_criteria: Existing FraterniTees lead scoring, score trends, account directory, account detail, map, and top-customer surfaces remain available.
Sales metrics use tenant-configured status, payment, tag, and dirty-data rules.
Email is draft-only with copy/open-client/mark-sent behavior.
Opportunities and reorders are tenant-scoped and auditable.
stop_conditions: Printavo write-back would be required.
Existing FraterniTees scoring would be removed or hidden without replacement.
safe_defaults: Treat Printavo as read-only.
Show reports as preliminary when mappings are incomplete.
Keep email draft-only.

items.title: Screenprinting Social MVP
items.description: Build the Screenprinting Social workspace: owned/watched accounts, manual/API imports, posts, calendar, alerts, comments, messages, campaigns, conversations, and social-to-sales links.
acceptance_criteria: Owned and watched accounts support multiple accounts per tenant.
Manual import works when provider API credentials or permissions are unavailable.
Alerts are configurable per tenant.
Comments, replies, and messages can be linked to customers, organizations, contacts, opportunities, and campaigns.
Publishing remains disabled unless separately feature flagged.
stop_conditions: Live publishing would be required.
Social provider permissions are missing and no manual fallback can represent the workflow.
safe_defaults: Use manual import.
Disable publishing.
Show permission-required states for comments and messages until configured.

items.title: Identity resolution and merge suggestions
items.description: Create non-destructive identity suggestions for linking Printavo customers, organizations, contacts, Instagram handles, messages, comments, and opportunities.
acceptance_criteria: Suggestions include evidence and confidence.
Confirm, reject, ignore, and undo paths are available.
No destructive merge happens without explicit review.
Tenant admins can adjust matching rules and dirty-field trust.
stop_conditions: A destructive merge is required.
Cross-tenant identity data would be exposed.
safe_defaults: Create suggestions instead of merges.
Mark low-confidence suggestions for review.
Preserve all source identities.

items.title: Screenprinting fixtures and acceptance coverage
items.description: Add realistic fixtures and acceptance scripts that prove mapping, dirty data, reorders, social alerts, identity links, and tenant isolation without requiring live provider credentials.
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures cover good, bad, great, and dirty tenant data.
Acceptance scripts prove tenant config changes affect reports and workflows.
Tests prove FraterniTees and PICC data remain isolated.
Live-provider tests are optional and skipped cleanly when credentials are absent.
stop_conditions: A live provider credential is required with no fixture fallback.
safe_defaults: Use committed fixtures.
Skip live tests with explicit skip messages when credentials are missing.

items.title: Screenprinting operational UI browser verification
items.description: Correct the Screenprinting UI so real tenants do not see fake KPI fallbacks, inert controls, or unlabeled demo data. Verify the FraterniTees browser path against live Printavo-backed data.
acceptance_criteria: FraterniTees Sales dashboard shows real synced Printavo totals, revenue, AOV, customer counts, mapped status buckets, mapped payment buckets, and reorder counts.
Orders table and order detail modal use real tenant order rows and clearly withhold profitability until catalog/decorator costs are reliable.
Social dashboard shows real data or explicit empty/permission states; no demo social rows appear for real saved tenants.

Manual social import, manual thread logging, campaign planning, alert updates, comments/replies, and identity resolution are exposed as operating surfaces with API-backed or explicit disabled/error behavior.
Every visible button either calls a tenant-scoped API, opens a local workflow, copies/opens draft text, or is disabled with a reason.
stop_conditions: A surface needs provider write-back to appear functional.
A real tenant path would require unlabeled fixture data.
safe_defaults: Use real tenant data or explicit unavailable states.
Keep Printavo read-only.
Keep social publishing disabled.
Label fixture/demo/manual-source rows clearly.

items.title: Second Screenprinting tenant onboarding proof
items.description: Prove a second Screenprinting tenant can inherit the Screenprinting tenant type, override config, connect/import data, and use Sales/Social without a code fork.
acceptance_criteria: Second tenant workspace manifest exists as fixture or seed.
Tenant override diff is visible.
Sales and Social dashboards reflect that tenant's own mappings and accounts.
No shared-code tenant branch is added.
stop_conditions: The proof would require tenant-specific code.
safe_defaults: Use fixture tenant data.
Use manual provider imports if credentials are unavailable.

items.title: Catalog adapter foundation
items.description: Add a real catalog adapter boundary for tenant-selected vendor cost APIs after the order/customer foundation is stable.
acceptance_criteria: Tenant can choose a catalog provider adapter.
Cost data remains optional and warning-gated.
Profitability features remain disabled until reliable data exists.
stop_conditions: Profitability would be built on incomplete cost data.
safe_defaults: Keep profitability disabled.
Show missing-cost warnings.

items.title: Profitability and cost reporting
items.description: Add profitability only after orders, customers, line items, and catalog costs are reliable enough for tenant decision-making.
acceptance_criteria: Profitability calculations document every input and exclusion.
Dirty or missing cost data prevents authoritative profitability claims.
Tenant can configure catalog source and trusted fields.
stop_conditions: Required cost data is missing or unreliable.
safe_defaults: Disable profitability widgets.
Show cost coverage instead of margin when data is incomplete.

items.title: Screenprinting art workflow module
items.description: Add art/proof/assets workflow after Sales and Social are stable.
acceptance_criteria: Art workflow has its own self-contained product spec before implementation.
Assets, approvals, proofs, and licensing states are defined.
Module can be feature-flagged per tenant.
stop_conditions: Sales/Social foundation is not stable.
safe_defaults: Keep art module disabled until specified.

items.title: Screenprinting warehouse workflow module
items.description: Add warehouse/inventory/fulfillment workflow after Sales and Social are stable.
acceptance_criteria: Warehouse workflow has its own self-contained product spec before implementation.
Inventory, SKU, receiving, fulfillment, and exception states are defined.
Module can be feature-flagged per tenant.
stop_conditions: Sales/Social foundation is not stable.
safe_defaults: Keep warehouse module disabled until specified.

items.title: Future stack extraction proposal
items.description: Evaluate physical package boundaries, pnpm/Turborepo, schema-per-vertical, custom migration tooling, branch databases, generators, and spinout drills after product pressure proves the need.
acceptance_criteria: A separate approved migration plan exists.
Current tenant behavior is verified before topology changes.
Migration has rollback notes and incremental checkpoints.
stop_conditions: The migration would pause current tenant delivery without approval.
The migration would require broad package manager or repository topology changes without a separate plan.
safe_defaults: Do not start broad topology migration.
Promote only the smallest useful foundation slice when it blocks a current ready item.
